summary: Remote-eval dropper in @sqlite-node/createsql fetches and executes arbitrary GitHub Gist content on require
details: On require/import, index.js fetches https://api.github.com/gists/198a0bbec7a6018e9250615d26e37b90, extracts the first file's content, and passes it to eval(). The gist is mutable and controlled by GitHub user metabrainai000, so any JavaScript that user chooses to host runs in the installer's Node process with full privileges. There is no integrity check, no version pin, and the fetched content is not validated. The package metadata advertises a SQLite toolkit but ships no SQLite code — only the remote-eval dropper — and the package.json repository field points to an unrelated GitHub repo (guilderguzman/array-utl_nodelump), with a typo'd description ("SQLite Tooslkit"). The scope/name appear designed to lure developers searching for SQLite tooling into pulling an arbitrary-code-execution payload.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
